TEST.0.title: Codebusters 2026 Division B Regional Test # 5
TEST.0.customHeader:
Names of participants: (Please print neatly)

 
 
 
School Name:

Check your team:  ◻ Varsity     ◻ JV1     ◻ JV2     ◻ JV3

Warning: Do not open this packet until given permission to do so.

Note: There are useful notes after this page.

CIPHER.390.cipherString: It's easier to change what you do than people think it is. If you don't change, your field changes around you.
CIPHER.390.question: Someone left this coded message in the school lab. You are told it's a quote by Walter Gilbert encoded with an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.390.author: Walter Gilbert

CIPHER.373.cipherString: Night had come on like the closing of a great but gentle eye.
CIPHER.373.question: The following odd symbols were found when a tomb was opened, but you recognized it as the handiwork of a prankster who scratched a quote by Ray Bradbury on the wall using a Baconian alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.373.author: Ray Bradbury

CIPHER.367.cipherString: The ego relies on the familiar. It is reluctant to experience the unknown, which is they very essence of life.
CIPHER.367.question: You find this encoded line written in invisible ink on a lab safety poster. It's a quote by Deepak Chopra, written in an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.367.author: Deepak Chopra

CIPHER.378.cipherString: The technicians thought I was crazy. Now, five months later, I've proved it.
CIPHER.378.question: Found on a microchip inside a disassembled robotics kit: decode this Porta Cipher by James Blish using a keyword of DRUM. What does it say?
CIPHER.378.author: James Blish

CIPHER.379.cipherString: Acting is magical. Change your look and your attitude, and you can be anyone.
CIPHER.379.question: A mysterious postcard arrives from an unknown sender. It contains a coded quote by Alicia Witt, written in a Porta using a keyword of BRICK. What does it say?
CIPHER.379.author: Alicia Witt

CIPHER.380.cipherString: He will win who knows how to handle both superior and inferior forces.
CIPHER.380.question: Hidden in the source code of a retro video game, this Complete Columnar puzzle reveals a quote from Total War: Shogun 2. The quote has RFOR somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.380.author: Total War: Shogun 2

CIPHER.368.cipherString: Long ago I discovered that there was more to life than cricket, and more to cricket than runs and wickets.
CIPHER.368.question: David Foot offers us some advice that has been encoded as an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher starts with LONG. What does it say?
CIPHER.368.author: David Foot

CIPHER.387.cipherString: Art is the daughter of freedom.
CIPHER.387.question: A note falls out of a borrowed library book. The message is a quote by Friedrich Schiller, written as an Affine Cipher with a=17 and b=18. What does it say?
CIPHER.387.author: Friedrich Schiller

CIPHER.388.cipherString: Poetry is what is lost in translation. It is also what is lost in interpretation.
CIPHER.388.question: A coded message appears after you shine a UV light on a classroom poster. It turns out to be a quote by Robert Frost, encrypted using a Checkerboard with a polybius key of SOUVENIR. What does it say?
CIPHER.388.author: Robert Frost

CIPHER.374.cipherString: Beware of the man who won't be bothered with details.
CIPHER.374.question: A mysterious QR code on the back of a cereal box leads to this encrypted quote by William Feather using the Fractionated Morse Cipher. You are told that the quote has EBOTH in it corresponding to the encoded text R S U I W B A, meaning A = ●×●; B = ●●●; I = –––; R = ×●×; S = ×–●; U = ●●×; W = ×–×. What does it say?
CIPHER.374.author: William Feather

CIPHER.372.cipherString: I have a vest. If I had my arms cut off, it would be a jacket.
CIPHER.372.question: A mysterious app update added this Baconian lock screen—it's actually Mitch Hedberg's quote. What does it say?
CIPHER.372.author: Mitch Hedberg

CIPHER.383.cipherString: Technology is anything that wasn't around when you were born.
CIPHER.383.question: Frozen inside an ice core sample from Antarctica: this Nihilist Substitution message by Alan Kay with a keyword of OBJECT and polybius key of MICROBES. What does it say?
CIPHER.383.author: Alan Kay

CIPHER.371.cipherString: Experiment is an art, and demands an artist.
CIPHER.371.question: An explorer's journal mentions a “hidden truth” encoded below. It turns out to be a quote by Aristotle: a Chapter from the History of Science, written in a Baconian Cipher. You are told that the deciphered text starts with a single letter followed by XPER. What does it say?
CIPHER.371.author: Aristotle: a Chapter from the History of Science

CIPHER.377.question: A mysterious postcard arrives from an unknown sender. It contains a coded quote, written in a Cryptarithm. What do the values 3811478 9538602 decode to?

CIPHER.369.cipherString: There is nothing more beautiful than cheerfulness in an old face, and among country people it is always a sign of a well-regulated life.
CIPHER.369.question: You stumble across this encoded text on an old floppy disk labeled “Project Discovery.” It's a quote by Richter encoded as a Patristocrat using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher ends with LIFE. What does it say?
CIPHER.369.author: Richter

CIPHER.382.cipherString: I don't have an attitude; I have a personality you can't handle.
CIPHER.382.question: This string of symbols appears on the display of a malfunctioning calculator. It hides a quote, encoded as a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of MAYOR and polybius key of CAMPSITE. What does it say?
CIPHER.382.author: Unknown

CIPHER.375.cipherString: Knowledge teaches me to run, but wisdom teaches me to walk.
CIPHER.375.question: You receive this puzzling text during a live science broadcast. It contains a quote by Anthony T. Hincks, encrypted as a Fractionated Morse. You are told that the quote has OWLEDG in it corresponding to the encoded text W L K B E V A G, meaning A = ●×–; B = ●–●; E = ●×●; G = –●×; K = ––×; L = –×●; V = ×–●; W = ×––. What does it say?
CIPHER.375.author: Anthony T. Hincks

CIPHER.384.cipherString: Keep your best wishes, close to your heart and watch what happens
CIPHER.384.question: A mysterious balloon release at the science fair carried this Atbash banner by Tony DeLiso. What does it say?
CIPHER.384.author: Tony DeLiso

CIPHER.381.cipherString: Not everything in life is achievable by a straight path.
CIPHER.381.question: Someone passed you a piece of paper with this Complete Columnar encoded quote by Priyanka Agarwal. The quote has IFEISAC somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.381.author: Priyanka Agarwal

CIPHER.385.cipherString: Nothing puzzles me more than time and space; and yet nothing troubles me less, as I never think about them.
CIPHER.385.question: You stumble across this encoded text on an old floppy disk labeled “Project Discovery.” It's a quote by Charles Lamb encoded as a Caesar. What does it say?
CIPHER.385.author: Charles Lamb

CIPHER.389.cipherString: The shortness of life, so often lamented, may be the best thing about it.
CIPHER.389.question: A strange code appears in the footer of a document you print from the lab computer. It's a quote by Arthur Schopenhauer, encoded in a Checkerboard with a polybius key of DIRECTLY. What does it say?
CIPHER.389.author: Arthur Schopenhauer

CIPHER.386.question: Your solar-powered calculator started flashing this Affine sequence—it's Chris Guillebeau's words with a=11 and b=23. What does it say?
CIPHER.386.author: Chris Guillebeau

CIPHER.366.cipherString: Bunsen was no theorist, and purely speculative questions had little or no interest for him.
CIPHER.366.question: A riddle is discovered taped under a chemistry lab bench. It's a quote, encrypted as an Aristocrat. What does it say?

CIPHER.370.cipherString: Sé que muchos artistas sienten que son unos impostores; eso forma parte del placer de la creatividad.
CIPHER.370.question: A coded message is discovered inside a fortune cookie. It turns out to be a quote by David Cronenberg in Spanish, encoded using a Xenocrypt Cipher using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains UNOS. What does it say?
CIPHER.370.author: David Cronenberg
CIPHER.370.translation: I know that many artists feel that they are frauds - that's part of the pleasure of creativity.

CIPHER.376.question: Your drone photographed some weird markings in a parking lot. Turns out it's a quote in a Cryptarithm. What do the values 7318 4693165 2619246 5608 decode to?
